subtasks.title: Initialize project and dependencies
subtasks.description: Create project directory, initialize npm, set up git, and install core dependencies (express, nodemon, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer) and a simple build script.
subtasks.successCriteria: Repository created with package.json, git initialized, and required dependencies listed in package.json; npm scripts for dev and build exist.

subtasks.title: Set up Express server and static serving
subtasks.description: Create an Express server file that serves static assets from a public folder and provides a base route for index.html; include hot-reload dev script (nodemon) for development.
subtasks.successCriteria: Express server starts with npm run dev and serves index.html and static assets from the public directory on the configured port.

subtasks.title: Configure Tailwind CSS build pipeline
subtasks.description: Install and configure Tailwind via CLI/PostCSS: create tailwind.config.js, postcss.config.js (or use Tailwind CLI build), and a source CSS that includes Tailwind directives; set up npm build to output public/styles.css.
subtasks.successCriteria: Tailwind is configured and a compiled CSS file (public/styles.css) is generated that contains Tailwind utilities when the build script runs.

subtasks.title: Create base HTML and static asset structure
subtasks.description: Add public/index.html, public/scripts.js and reference compiled Tailwind CSS; create folders for assets and ensure index.html contains a container for the editable 'hello world' text.
subtasks.successCriteria: index.html loads in the browser, references public/styles.css and public/scripts.js, and displays the default 'hello world' text area.

subtasks.title: Build frontend UI controls with Tailwind
subtasks.description: Implement a small UI in index.html (and scripts.js) including a text input for the message, color pickers for text and background, and preview area styled with Tailwind classes for responsiveness.
subtasks.successCriteria: UI shows editable text input, color pickers, and a live preview that updates visually when inputs change (without backend integration).

subtasks.title: Implement Express API to get/save settings
subtasks.description: Create lightweight API routes (e.g., GET /api/settings and POST /api/settings) that store the hello text and color choices in-memory (or JSON file) and return current settings.
subtasks.successCriteria: API endpoints respond with JSON and accept updates; POST persists to in-memory store (or file) and GET returns the latest values.

subtasks.title: Connect UI to API and persist changes
subtasks.description: Add frontend JS to fetch initial settings from GET /api/settings, apply them to the preview, and POST updates when the user changes text or colors; handle success/error feedback.
subtasks.successCriteria: Changing inputs updates the preview and a save triggers a successful POST; reloading the page reflects saved settings from the backend.

subtasks.title: Test functionality and edge cases
subtasks.description: Manually and minimally automate tests: verify server starts, static files serve, API responds correctly, UI updates preview, color values valid, and persistence works across reloads.
subtasks.successCriteria: All manual test cases pass: server serves UI, API returns saved settings, UI updates preview, and inputs are validated; basic automated tests (if created) pass.

subtasks.title: Write README and run instructions
subtasks.description: Create README with setup, dev, and build instructions, describe the API endpoints and how to modify text/colors; include commands to run the project locally.
subtasks.successCriteria: README contains clear steps to install, run, build, and test the app so another developer can reproduce the environment and use the UI to change text/colors.

decompositionStrategy: Break the project into environment setup, server configuration, CSS toolchain, static frontend, UI components, API, integration, testing, and documentation to ensure incremental, testable progress with clear dependencies.
